description: Between the upper and lower portions of a leg, 'kunoa' is the joint that bends and bears weight. 'tolae' (leg) covers the whole limb rather than this one joint.
articulatory_notes: Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for unvoiced 'k' and release into high-back rounded 'u'. Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for dental 'n', let the voice pass through the nose, and move into stressed middle-height back rounded 'o'. Release the contact, unround the lips, and lower the jaw into open central 'a'.

semantic_domains.physical: the bending joint in the middle of a leg
